title: Amendment and recodification of Title 15 and 17 of the American Fork Municipal Code
description: Public hearing on amendment and recodification of Title 15 and 17 of the American Fork Municipal Code. The amendment looks to recodify and identify the existing conditions, the planning and visioning process, zoning, preferred development objectives, preferred development scenario, and implementation strategies within the municipal code.
notes: Comprehensive municipal code updates affecting planning and zoning regulations. Draft documents available in Planning Commission Packet at www.AmericanFork.gov

title: Update to the American Fork General Plan
description: Public hearing on an update to the American Fork General Plan to update all sections to establish an updated vision for growth and development in the City.
notes: Comprehensive general plan update affecting citywide growth and development vision. Draft documents available in Planning Commission Packet at www.AmericanFork.gov

title: Adoption and Amendment of Design Standards and Public Improvement Specifications
description: American Fork City Planning Commission will consider proposed amendment and adoption of Design Standards and Public Improvement Specifications applicable to development within the city. The proposal includes removing existing engineering standards from the Municipal Code and adopting new standards by reference, aligned with Utah APWA Standard Specifications while establishing uniform city-specific design criteria.
notes: Infrastructure standards amendment affecting all future development in city. Planning Commission will recommend to City Council.

title: Jasper Lot Land Use Map Amendment at 570 E Main Street

location: 570 E Main Street, American Fork City
notes: Small lot conversion from residential to commercial use. Planning Commission will review and make recommendation to City Council.

location: 794 W 1000 N Circle, American Fork City
notes: Amended final plat for subdivision cleanup; public hearing scheduled March 2, 2026 at 9:00 AM at Public Works building, 275 East 200 North

title: 04.28.26 Work Session agenda
agenda_text:
AMERICAN FORK CITY COUNCIL
APRIL 28, 2026
		WORK SESSION AGENDA




*Notice of Electronic Meeting*
One or more City Council members may be physically absent from this meeting but may participate electronically.

Notice is hereby given that the American Fork City Council will hold a work session on Tuesday, April 28, 2026, in the City Administration Conference Room, located at 51 East Main Street, commencing at 4:00 p.m. The agenda shall be as follows:
WORK SESSION
The purpose of the City Work Sessions is to prepare the City Council for upcoming agenda items on future City Council Meetings. The Work Session is not an action item meeting. No one attending the meeting should rely on any discussion or any perceived consensus as action or authorization. These come only from the City Council Meeting.

1.	Discussion on the fiscal year 2027 General Fund, Capital Projects, Fleet Fund, and Fitness Center fund.
2.	Training in Ethics and Conflict of Interest.
3.	Adjourn. 

Dated the 23rd day of April 2026.

/s/Terilyn Lurker
City Recorder









-	In accordance with the Americans with Disabilities Act, the City of American Fork will make reasonable accommodations to participate in the meeting. Requests for assistance can be made by contacting the City Recorder at 801-763-3000 at least 48 hours in advance of the meeting.
-	The order of agenda items may be changed to accommodate the needs of the City Council, staff, and the public.

title: Williams Development Agreement for natural gas power facility at Stadion parcel to support Meta data center expansion
description: Williams, an independent power producer, seeks to co-locate a natural gas power generation facility on the Stadion parcel (zoned RTI) as an accessory use to the Meta data center expansion. The development agreement clarifies rights and responsibilities between Williams and the City. Williams requests code exceptions including: (1) sound level increase to 75 dB (exceeding city nighttime limit of 65 dB, but consistent with neighboring industrial standards), and (2) permission to use above-ground power distribution lines. Williams responsible for public infrastructure installation.
notes: This is accessory infrastructure supporting the larger Meta data center expansion. Sound variance request (75 dB vs 65 dB limit) and above-ground power lines are key negotiated exceptions. Project requires development agreement approval rather than standard permitting.

title: Public Hearing
agenda_text:
EAGLE MOUNTAIN CITY COUNCIL
NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARINGS

The Eagle Mountain City Council will hold public hearings on April 21, 2026, at 7:00 PM or as soon thereafter as business allows, in the Eagle Mountain City Council Chambers, 1650 E Stagecoach Run, Eagle Mountain, Utah 84005.  The City Council will receive public comments concerning the following items:

Amendments to Eagle Mountain Municipal Code:

-	EMMC Chapters 17.60 and 17.10 Fencing Amendment: Consideration of amendments to Eagle Mountain Municipal Code Sections 17.60.120 and 17.10.030 to add 'pre-weathered weathering steel' as an approved material type for residential perimeter fencing and to define 'pre-weathered weathering steel fences.' Initiated by Eagle Mountain Resident, Katie Elliott. This item was tabled by City Council for updates on 3/3/2026 EMC Point of Contact: Planner, Mickie Mills mmills@eaglemountain.gov. 

-	EMMC Chapter 17.72.040: Consideration of amendment to Eagle Mountain Municipal Code Section 17.72.040 to replace existing provisions with more specific standards for the screening of outdoor HVAC condenser units installed for multi-family buildings. EMC Point of Contact: Senior Planner, Robert Hobbs: rhobbs@eaglemountain.gov. 

Materials for public hearing items may be viewed within three days of the meeting on the city website and at the Eagle Mountain City Recorder's Office, 1650 E Stagecoach Run, Eagle Mountain, UT, during normal business hours, Monday through Friday, 7:30 AM to 5:30 PM. If you need special accommodation to participate in the meeting, please call the City Recorder's Office at 801.789.6611.  Open portions of the meeting can be viewed at www.eaglemountain.gov. 

THE PUBLIC IS INVITED TO PARTICIPATE IN ALL CITY COUNCIL MEETINGS.

title: Erda City Council Emergency Meeting
agenda_text:
Notice is hereby given that the Erda City Council will hold an emergency meeting on Friday March 27th, 2026 at 6:30 PM hold a closed session pursuant to Utah Code §52-4-205(1) for a strategy session to discuss imminent litigation requiring immediate council direction. The meeting will be held at the Erda City Fire Station/Office, located at 2163 West Erda Way, Erda, Utah


Call to Order
Roll Call
Motion and Vote to Enter Closed Session (reason: imminent litigation requiring immediate council direction)
Conduct Closed Session
Adjournment 


**All handouts for this meeting can be found on the city's website erda.gov under the calendar section**

Board may elect to go into a closed session which will not be open to the public pursuant to Utah Code Ann. §52-4-205(1).
**Format and Verbiage to be used when going into a closed session
'I move that we temporarily recess the Council meeting to go into a closed meeting for a strategy session to discuss pending or reasonably imminent litigation.'
 
The motion needs a second and a roll call vote.
 
The Chair then makes this statement:   'The motion passes and the Council will go into closed meeting for a strategy session to discuss pending or reasonably imminent litigation that will be held in this room.'


Pursuant to the Americans with Disabilities Act, Individuals needing special accommodations should contact Larry Kimpel at 435-354-9216

title: Adoption of Ordinance 2026-12 updating sewer rate schedule for wastewater system funding through FY 2035
description: Grantsville City Council adopted Ordinance 2026-12 establishing an updated sewer rate schedule to fund operation, maintenance, and long-term sustainability of the City's wastewater system, including costs for a new wastewater treatment facility. The ordinance implements a multi-year rate structure through FY 2035 with increases to residential and non-residential base fees and usage-based fees beginning FY 2027, structured with higher initial increases followed by smaller annual increases to maintain financial reserves and meet debt service obligations.
notes: This ordinance signals infrastructure investment and capacity planning for wastewater treatment. The multi-year rate structure and new treatment facility indicate the city is planning for growth and increased demand through 2035.

title: Notice of Public Hearing
agenda_text:
GRANTSVILLE CITY NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING

PUBLIC NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN that the Grantsville City Council will hold a Public Hearing on Wednesday, April 15, 2026, at 7:00 p.m. for the purpose of receiving public input regarding a proposed budget amendment to the General Fund.

The proposed amendment will increase the Capital Projects line item within the Class C Roads Department budget in the amount of $3,037,000.00 for the purchase of road department equipment.

This amendment reflects the use of Class C Road Funds, which are restricted for roadway construction, maintenance, and improvement purposes. No new revenues or external funding sources are being proposed as part of this amendment.

The proposed equipment purchases are intended to improve operational efficiency, reduce long-term maintenance costs, and enhance the City's ability to maintain and preserve roadway infrastructure.

This meeting will be held electronically via Zoom and in-person at Grantsville City Hall, located at 429 E. Main Street, Grantsville, Utah, 84029. Public comments are welcome and may be made in person or by participating through Zoom using the following:
Meeting URL: https://us02web.zoom.us/j/4358843411?omn=89242001869 Dial-In: 1-253-215-8782 Meeting ID: 435 884 3411

Public comments may also be submitted via email to the City Recorder, Alicia Fairbourne, at afairbourne@grantsvilleut.gov. All comments will be distributed to the City Council and entered into the public record. Comments must be received prior to 12:00 p.m. (noon) on the day of the meeting in order to be considered.

Copies of the proposed resolution and supporting materials are available for public review at the Grantsville City Offices during regular business hours and as part of the City Council meeting materials posted on the City's official website and the Utah Public Notice Website.

DATED this 8th day of April, 2026

Alicia Fairbourne Grantsville City Recorder

title: Public Hearing Notice for the Lehi City Community Development Block Grant
agenda_text:
Public Hearing and Public Comment Period Notice
Lehi City Community Development Block Grant
Mountainland Association of Governments will hold a public hearing to consider feedback on the Lehi City CDBG Annual Action Plan, which outlines community needs, potential community funding priorities, and program structure and governance for the program year 2026-2027. The amount of CDBG funds for this program year will be discussed along with the range of projects eligible.
The hearing will be held in the Lehi Council Chambers located at 131 North 100 East, Lehi, Utah, on April 28th at 7:00 pm. Suggestions will be solicited verbally, electronically, and in writing from all interested parties during the public comment period from April 3 to May 5, 2026. To comment or for further information, contact Claudia Saldana at 801-229-3673 or csaldana@mountainland.org.
The public hearing facility is fully accessible to persons with disabilities. Individuals needing special accommodations (including auxiliary aids/services or translation) during the hearing should notify Claudia Saldana at least five days prior to the hearing. A Utah Relay telephone number or a language interpreter can be provided upon request.
________________________________________
Mountainland Association of Governments llevará a cabo una audiencia pública para considerar la retroalimentación sobre el Plan de Acción Anual de la Ciudad de Lehi CDBG, que resume las necesidades de la comunidad, posibles prioridades de financiación comunitaria y la estructura y gestión del programa para el año 2026-2027. El monto estimado de los fondos CDBG para este año del programa se examinará junto con la gama de proyectos elegibles.
La audiencia se llevará a cabo en las oficinas de la ciudad de Lehi, 131 North 100 East, Lehi, Utah, el Martes 28 de Abril de 2026 a las 7:00 pm. Las sugerencias serán solicitadas verbalmente, electrónicamente y por escrito de todas las partes interesadas durante el período de comentarios públi

body: City of Saratoga Springs City Council
title: Public Hearing: Water Conservation Plan Update
agenda_text:
Notice of Public Hearing

The City of Saratoga Springs, Utah, proposes an update to the Water Conservation Plan ('Plan') pursuant to Utah Code Section 73-10-32. 

The City Council will hold a public hearing during its regular meeting on April 14th, 2026, beginning at 6:00 p.m. Held in the City Council Chambers at 319 S. Saratoga Road, Saratoga Springs UT, 84045, to discuss the proposed update to the Plan. 

Comments & questions about the Plan may be directed to the Public Works Dept. 801-766-6506. 

Comments may be submitted to comments@saratogasprings-ut.com.

body: City of Saratoga Springs Planning Commission
title: Amendment to Title 19 Land Development Code - Increase Maximum Building Footprint Size in Neighborhood Commercial Zone
description: City-wide amendment to Title 19 Chapter 19.04 to increase the Maximum Building Footprint Size in the Neighborhood Commercial Zone from 15,000 square feet to 60,000 square feet, allowing larger buildings in this zone.
notes: Developer-initiated code amendment affecting city-wide zoning regulations. Quadruples the maximum building footprint allowance (15k to 60k sq ft) in Neighborhood Commercial zones, which could enable larger commercial/mixed-use projects in these areas.

title: Creation of So Jo Marketplace Public Infrastructure District
description: Public hearing regarding the proposed creation of the So Jo Marketplace Public Infrastructure District. The City Council will consider whether to adopt a resolution creating the district and will allow public input on whether the requested services are needed in the area, whether services should be provided by the City or the District, and other related matters. All property owners and registered voters within the proposed district boundaries have already provided consent to creation and waived the protest period.
notes: Public Infrastructure District creation requires public hearing. All property owners and registered voters have consented to creation and waived protest period, allowing City Council to adopt resolution immediately after hearing or at any future date. District will have authority to adjust boundaries through annexation and withdrawal of properties within the proposed inclusion area.

title: Public Notice- Approving Budget Amendments for FY 2025-2026
agenda_text:
Public Notice

Notice is hereby given that the Tooele Council of Tooele City, Utah, will hold a Public Hearing to consider approving budget amendments for fiscal year 2025-2026. The hearing will be held on Wednesday, March 4, 2026, at 7:00 p.m. The meeting will be held at the Tooele City Hall Council Chambers, located at 90 North Main Street, Tooele, Utah.

Tooele City public meetings may be recorded and transcribed for documentation and quality assurance purposes. By attending this meeting, you consent to being recorded. If you do not consent, we encourage you to join the City Council meeting electronically by visiting the Tooele City YouTube Channel, at https://www.youtube.com/@tooelecity or by going to YouTube.com and searching 'Tooele City Channel'. If you are attending electronically and would like to submit a written comment for the public comment period or for a public hearing item, please email cmpubliccomment@tooelecity.gov. If submission by email is not an option, written comments may be submitted to the City Recorder. Written comments must be submitted no later than the day prior to the meeting. Written comments will be addressed at the designated points in the meeting.

Pursuant to the Americans with Disabilities Act, Individuals Needing Special Accommodations Should Notify Shilo Baker, Tooele City Recorder, at 435-843-2113 or shilob@tooelecity.gov, Prior to the Meeting.

March 17, 2026, City Council Agenda
NOTICE OF A REGULAR CITY COUNCIL MEETING
March 17, 2026, at 12:00 PM
________________
PUBLIC NOTICE is hereby given that the Vineyard City Council will hold a regularly scheduled City Council meeting on Tuesday, March 17, 2026, at 12:00 PM, in the City Council Chambers at City Hall, 125 South Main Street, Vineyard, UT. This meeting can also be viewed on our live stream page.
AGENDA
Presiding Mayor Zack Stratton

1. CALL TO ORDER/INVOCATION/INSPIRATIONAL THOUGHT/PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE

2. PUBLIC COMMENTS
'Public Comments' is defined as time set aside for citizens to express their views for items not on the agenda. During a period designated for public comment, the mayor or chair may allot each speaker a maximum amount of time to present their comments, subject to extension by the mayor or by a majority vote of the council. Speakers offering duplicate comments may be limited. Because of the need for proper public notice, immediate action cannot be taken in the Council Meeting. The Chair of the meeting reserves the right to organize public comments by topic and may group speakers accordingly. If action is necessary, the item will be listed on a future agenda; however, the Council may elect to discuss the item if it is an immediate matter of concern. Public comments can be submitted ahead of time to tonyl@vineyardutah.gov.

3.4. RDA Audit Update

3.6. Operation Neighborhood Shield

3.7. City Commissions, Appointment Processes, And Volunteer Recruitment

4. ADJOURNMENT

title: Public Hearing for the Consolidated Fee Schedule
agenda_text:
PUBLIC NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING
VINEYARD CITY COUNCIL

Notice is hereby given that the Vineyard City Council will hold a Public Hearing on Tuesday, April 28, 2026, at 6:00 p.m., at Vineyard City Hall, 125 South Main Street, Vineyard, Utah.

The purpose of the Public Hearing is to receive public comment regarding proposed amendments to the Vineyard City Consolidated Fee Schedule, including updates and modifications to fees associated with:
-	Parks and Recreation programs and services
-	Vineyard Days events and activities
-	Library services

All interested persons are invited to attend the hearing and provide comment. Written comments may also be submitted to the City Recorder prior to the meeting at robinr@vineyardutah.gov

In compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act, individuals needing special accommodations (including auxiliary communicative aids and services) should notify the City Recorder at least three (3) business days prior to the hearing. 801-226-1929

I, the undersigned City Recorder for Vineyard, Utah, hereby certify that the foregoing Public Notice as posted at the Vineyard City Office, on the Vineyard City website, and on the Utah Public Notice Website.

Published this 13 day of April 2026.
Robin Bond, Vineyard City Recorder
